author: kevaundray
content: Any hiccups or was it pretty straightforward?

author: taulepton_
content: Surprisingly worked first time!

author: kevaundray
content: For running it locally on kurtosis, we are using the branches that pandaops automatically creates docker images for?

author: taulepton_
content: Yes I am using ethpandaops images for both lh and prysm in Kurtosis

author: taulepton_
content: Both built from the optional proofs branch

author: kevaundray
content: Great work!

author: manunlp
content:
Prysm actually only works in the "nominal case".
For example, if you try to sync a prysm node after genesis, it won't fetch proofs via RPC ATM.
(In progress)

author: kevaundray
content: whats the nominal case?

author: taulepton_
content: All proofs arrive via gossip

author: taulepton_
content: I think this is fine for first interop devnet

author: taulepton_
content: We will do rpc sync in a follow up devnet

author: barnabasbusa
content: do we wanna launch something more stable ?

author: kevaundray
content: <@531934490826768418>  To clarify, This means it will work for local kurtosis devnets where everyone starts from block 0, but a prysm node won't work if it joins the network after genesis or if it falls behind and needs to sync?

author: taulepton_
content: Yes probably. Might be cool to land the no EL validators first? What do you think?

author: manunlp
content:
If all proofs arrive via gossip **after** the block.
ATM I don't memoize proofs received before the block and don't try to fetch them after either.
So really it works if everything is "nominal"

author: manunlp
content: exactly

author: kevaundray
content: Do we know when we would be able to have Prysm work in the non-genesis case?

author: taulepton_
content: <@531934490826768418> will it eventually sync the network when old blocks are finalised by the network?
